hyperedges.label: Codex Project Overlay Shared by All .codex Skills
graphify Full Pipeline (Detect, Extract, Build, Cluster, Export, Update)
G13 Auth Dual Migration: Magic Link to OTP+Google
Codex Project Overlay shared by frontend-design and next-best-practices skills

Modal pattern via parallel + intercepting routes
Next.js Cache Lifecycle Management

Postgres Concurrency and Locking Practices
Index Optimization Strategy Group
RLS Security and Performance Pattern

Routine template/day/item data model used across architecture flows and DATABASE.md
Shared Project Overlay rules across all .codex/subagents/*.md files (AGENTS.md, ROUTING_GRAPH, FILE_OWNERSHIP)

G1-architecture specs resolve into docs/architecture/*.md
docs/codex/ tactical routing layer files form a navigation system
Shell, panel lateral and navegacion form the unified user/admin experience pattern
Exercise Modal Cross-View Integration
MVP End-to-End QA Validation
MVP Closure Documentation Update

Convergence of OTP and Google identities into single profile/role model
External configuration documentation for OTP and Google providers
Estructura semanal de rutinas: routine_templates -> routine_days -> routine_items

Cadena de implementacion: DATABASE.md (G2) traducido a SQL concreto en G2.1
Pipeline de migracion G2: constraints -> SQL final -> aplicar -> documentar
Decision pipeline G3: identificar, filtrar, acotar y documentar skills/agentes

G4.5 sequential decision chain consolidated into 07-frontend-experience.md
Shell + views implementation and validation cycle
Auth + role integration spanning G5 and shell admin access
G5 admin access control layer (layout guards + server guards + redirects)
RLS admin helper strategy shared across profiles, catalog, and admin writes
G6 exercise admin pipeline (model, validation, images, listing view)
Routine persistence chain: templates -> days -> items -> exercises
Admin routine builder flow: form, multi-day, multi-row support
Catalog detail to exercise modal navigation flow

Flujo de dashboard, listado y detalle de rutina guardada con modal de ejercicio

nodes.label: Auth Dual: OTP Email + Google OAuth
nodes.norm_label: auth dual: otp email + google oauth

nodes.label: upgrade codemod (middleware to proxy)
nodes.rationale: Next.js 16 renamed middleware.ts/middleware() to proxy.ts/proxy() for clarity, same capabilities; codemod upgrade automates the rename.

nodes.label: Preload Pattern (cache + preload)
nodes.rationale: Use React cache() to memoize data fetcher and a preload() fire-and-forget call to start fetching early so data is likely ready by the time it's awaited, avoiding waterfalls.

nodes.label: Promise.all Parallel Fetching
nodes.rationale: Avoids sequential waterfalls by fetching independent data concurrently with Promise.all instead of awaiting each call in sequence.

nodes.label: Streaming with Suspense
nodes.rationale: Wrap independent data-fetching sections in separate Suspense boundaries so each section streams in progressively rather than blocking the whole page.

nodes.label: React cache() for metadata dedup
nodes.rationale: Use React cache() to memoize a data-fetching function so generateMetadata and the page component share one fetch instead of duplicating the request.

nodes.label: router.back() Modal Close Pattern
nodes.rationale: Modals implemented via intercepting routes must be closed with router.back(), not router.push() or <Link>, because push/Link add new history entries, don't clear the intercepted route, and can cause the modal to flash or persist; back() removes the intercepted route from history and properly unmounts the modal.

nodes.label: default.tsx Requirement for Parallel Slots
nodes.rationale: Every parallel route slot (e.g. @modal) must have a default.tsx returning null; without it, refreshing any page 404s because Next.js cannot determine what to render in that slot.

nodes.label: dynamic import with ssr:false
nodes.rationale: For client-only packages that reference window/document, wrap the import in next/dynamic with ssr:false to avoid ReferenceError in Server Components.

nodes.label: serverExternalPackages config
nodes.rationale: Packages with native bindings (sharp, bcrypt, canvas) or bundling issues should be externalized from the server bundle via serverExternalPackages rather than bundled.

nodes.label: unstable_rethrow for navigation errors
nodes.rationale: redirect()/notFound()/forbidden()/unauthorized() throw special internal errors that Next.js handles; wrapping them in try-catch can swallow these, so use unstable_rethrow(error) inside catch blocks to re-throw Next.js internal navigation errors while still handling real errors.

nodes.label: Custom Cache Handler for Multi-Instance ISR
nodes.rationale: ISR's default filesystem cache breaks across multiple instances because each instance has its own disk; a custom cacheHandler (Redis/S3) provides shared storage so revalidatePath/Tag and ISR are consistent across instances.
nodes.norm_label: custom cache handler for multi-instance isr

nodes.label: app/admin layout server-side guard (type_rol=admin)
nodes.rationale: Centralizar la proteccion de app/admin/ en un layout server-side que exige autenticacion y type_rol=admin, evitando depender de la UI para bloquear el acceso.

nodes.label: app/dashboard layout server-side guard (auth required, no admin)
nodes.rationale: Centralizar la proteccion de app/dashboard/ con un layout server-side que exige sesion autenticada pero no rol admin, dejando el dashboard accesible para usuarios comunes y admins.
nodes.norm_label: app/dashboard layout server-side guard (auth required, no admin)

nodes.label: requireUser / requireAdmin server guards
nodes.rationale: Base reutilizable de guards server-side para sostener proteccion de escritura administrativa en G6 y G7, sin depender de ocultar botones en la UI.

nodes.label: Estrategia de redirecciones por estado y rol
nodes.rationale: Visitante a login para rutas privadas; usuario comun de /admin a /dashboard; usuario autenticado desde /auth/login hacia /admin o /dashboard segun rol.
nodes.norm_label: estrategia de redirecciones por estado y rol

nodes.label: Bootstrap admin verificable (magic link + promocion type_rol=admin)
nodes.rationale: Ingresar una cuenta por magic link, verificar creacion automatica de profiles, promocionar manualmente type_rol='admin' en Supabase o MCP, y volver a entrar para validar la experiencia admin.
nodes.norm_label: bootstrap admin verificable (magic link + promocion type_rol=admin)

nodes.label: private.current_profile_id() helper
nodes.rationale: Helper interno para reutilizar el criterio de identidad sin recursiones de policy.

nodes.label: private.is_current_user_admin() helper
nodes.rationale: Helper interno para reconocer admin a partir de profiles.type_rol sin recursiones de policy; reutilizado para cerrar escrituras administrativas globales.

nodes.label: private.can_update_own_profile(...) helper
nodes.rationale: Bloquea cambios de type_rol desde clientes al actualizar el propio perfil.
